titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University, Photographs
Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University, Photographs
titlestmt.author: compiled by Manuscripts and Archives Staff

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 7, 2021 at 10:07

did.unittitle: Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University, photographs
did.abstract: The materials consist of photographs and pictures of Sterling Memorial Library at Yale. Includes views of interiors, exteriors, decoration, and construction.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 46-B (YRG 46-B), Records and materials documenting individual Yale buildings, facilities, and grounds.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sterling Memorial Library, Yale University, Photographs (RU 696). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged in three series and subsequent accessions. I. Construction Progress Photographs, 1927-1930. II. Interiors, Exteriors, and Decoration, circa 1927-1969. III. Negatives, circa 1930s-1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of photographs and pictures of Sterling Memorial Library at Yale. Includes views of interiors, exteriors, decoration, and construction.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Service entrance to shipping, Wall Street
Corner of High and Wall Streets
Crossing, detail: screen and arch to Cloister
Main Reading Room to North End
Main Reading Room to South End
Manuscripts and Archives reading room, details: exhibition cases
Manuscripts and Archives, detail: arch to Gutenburg Room
Microtext Reading Room (Periodicals Room until ca. 1970)
Asia Reading Room, Room 219 (American Lit. Collection)
Arts of the Book Room, Room 177 (Memorabilia)
[J. G. Rogers'] Scrapbooks for ideas, volume 1
[J. G. Rogers'] Scrapbooks for ideas, volume 2
Moldings: scene panels, label stops, etc.
Librarian's Court, L and B windows, carvings
Detail, Yellin gate, Manuscripts and Archives
Drawing of stained glass window, Exhibit Corridor medallion replacement EC-1, by Frederick Cole
View of High Street facade--James Gamble Rogers (drawing)
Photographs of the Franke Family Reading Room in Sterling Memorial Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs taken by Michael Marsland, Yale University Office of Public Information.

did.unittitle: Two Philosophers Discussing the Cosmos , Maffei
Entrance porch details: stone webs; bosses; sea monsters, ship, compass; iron grilles in front of radiators by Samuel Yellin
Peru and South America map stained window
Coronation book of Tsar Nicholas II
Recent acquisitions of Arabic and Islamic Reading Room
Stained glass windows, woodwork, and special collection material
Photographs of stonework removed from exterior of Sterling Memorial Library
Exterior stonework removed from Sterling Memorial Library

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.
